group.value: Group D — opponents Paraguay, Australia, Türkiye

wc_role.value: Co-host nation (with Canada and Mexico); tournament runs June 11 – July 19 across USA/Canada/Mexico

opener.value: Open Group D vs Paraguay on June 12, 2026 at SoFi Stadium, Inglewood/Los Angeles, California

opener_result.value: USA 4-1 Paraguay (Group D opener, June 12, 2026, SoFi Stadium/Los Angeles). Goals: Damián Bobadilla own goal 7' (off a McKennie–Pulisic move), Folarin Balogun 31' (assisted by Pulisic) and 45' (top-corner finish with his weaker left), 3-0 at halftime; Maurício pulled one back for Paraguay 73'; Gio Reyna 90+8' sealed 4-1. Pulisic was the architect of the first half and was subbed at halftime (Pochettino's call, no injury). It was the USA's first multi-goal World Cup win in 24 years (since 2002) and Pochettino's first WC match in charge.

key_players.value: Christian Pulisic (AC Milan, 84 caps, on-field leader), Tyler Adams, Weston McKennie, Tim Ream, Matt Turner (GK), Tim Weah

squad_news.value: Pochettino named the 26-man roster on 26 May 2026: 13 returnees from the 2022 World Cup plus 13 first-timers — the fifth-youngest squad the USA has ever sent to a World Cup.
